what_it_does: Implements point-in-time session cloning into a supervised live session and channel fabric: materializes a source transcript at a cut (compact:N, uuid:, ts:), spawns it via the session supervisor, registers the clone as a supervised channel member, supports DM via supervisor /inject+/watch, provides an operator-gated interactive launch command, runs a reflect-before-brief onboarding protocol, selects ollama models based on context size, assigns provenance-stable clone:// addresses, and manages clone lifecycle.
observations: Imports materialize_at_point, resume_cwd_for, and build_timeline from runtime.session_pointintime.
Maintains two launch paths: supervised+inject for agent use and an operator shell command for interactive channel membership.
Writes channel-member registry entries under .data/channels with transport:'supervised' so dispatch can push via the supervisor.
Onboarding is split into Phase 1/2 orient+reflect first, then Phase 3 bring-current only after the reflection is captured.
Context-size-aware ollama selection defaults to kimi-k2.7-code:cloud and falls back to deepseek-v4-flash:cloud for contexts above 256K tokens.
clone_address builds a provenance-stable URI from source_sid and at; get_by_address resolves it by scanning the clone registry.
Persists the clone's reflection and clone:// address back into the durable clone record.
Fleet onboarding runs in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor.
list_clones optionally prunes records whose supervisor session has closed.
end_clone tears down the supervisor session, deletes the materialized prefix, and deregisters the channel member.
prepare_at only materializes and emits the operator launch command without spawning anything.
standouts: The safety split between agent-launched supervised clones and operator-launched interactive channel members.
Reflect-before-brief onboarding designed to preserve the un-drifted perspective of the clone's era.
Context-aware ollama model fallback with explicit non-kimi override protection.
Provenance-based clone:// addressing that survives re-spawns of the same cut.
conventions: Uses COMPANY_SUPERVISOR_BASE env var with a localhost default.
Raises teaching-loud CloneError rather than returning silent failures.
Long narrative docstrings include dates, board references, and protocol document names.
Mixes public API functions with underscore-prefixed helpers.
Performs some imports inside functions (json in _estimate_context_tokens, concurrent.futures in onboard_fleet).
Hard-codes model names and context-window sizes as module constants.
concerns: _estimate_context_tokens uses a crude chars//4 heuristic and may overestimate; it is explicitly labeled a guardrail.
_sup parses error bodies as JSON without handling non-JSON error responses.
_alive_supervised conflates 'supervisor unreachable' with 'session not alive' by returning False on CloneError.
operator_launch_cmd interpolates cwd and description into a shell command without escaping.
onboard_fleet parallelizes /watch streams but does not address connection-pool or supervisor load limits.
get_by_address scans the entire clone registry linearly for each lookup.
notes: This is the clone-to-fabric runtime: it resurrects past session cuts as supervised, addressable channel members while keeping the interactive launch path human-gated.
questions: What are the exact request/response schemas for supervisor endpoints /spawn, /inject, /watch, /teardown, and /sessions?
How does the channel dispatch layer route pushes to transport:'supervised' members?
What guarantees does materialize_at_point provide that the source transcript remains byte-untouched?
How is the safety classifier enforced to block agent-launched interactive channels?
What is the relationship between clone:// and the mentioned mind:// addressing scheme?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual: the file materializes past sessions, launches supervised clones, registers them as channel members, and supports DM, onboarding, addressing, and lifecycle operations.
intention_signals: The file tries to build a clone fleet fabric where past versions of a session can be re-animated as live, addressable participants that advocate for their original intent, while keeping agent-unsafe interactive launches under operator control and routing large contexts to cheaper models.
novelty: Distinctive combination of point-in-time session resurrection, supervised (non-interactive) agent-safe clone launch, reflect-before-brief onboarding, provenance-based clone:// addresses, and context-size-driven ollama model fallback.
contribution: Provides a concrete runtime for spawning, addressing, messaging, onboarding, and tearing down point-in-time session clones; contributes the supervised-channel-member registry schema, the clone:// provenance address, the reflect-before-brief onboarding protocol, and the context-aware ollama model selection rule.
summary_for_embedding: Runtime module that materializes past session transcripts into supervised live clones, registers them as channel members, supports DM/onboarding/addressing, and gates interactive clone launch to operator control.
intention_for_embedding: Enable a fabric of addressable point-in-time selves that preserve era intent and can be consulted safely without an agent auto-launching interactive channels.

symbols.does: Exception class for loud clone failures.
symbols.description: Subclass of RuntimeError used throughout the module to raise teaching-loud errors instead of silent no-ops, for example when the supervisor is unreachable or a clone cannot be found.

symbols.does: Writes a supervised clone entry to the channel-member registry.
symbols.description: Creates a JSON file in .data/channels/<handle>.json containing handle, session_id, transport:'supervised', supervisor_session, supervisor_base, cwd, and description so the channel dispatch layer can push messages to it via the supervisor.

symbols.does: Removes a channel-member registry entry.
symbols.description: Deletes the .data/channels/<handle>.json file during teardown so the registry reflects actual presence.

symbols.does: HTTP helper for supervisor API calls.
symbols.description: Sends JSON requests to the session supervisor base URL, parses JSON responses, and raises CloneError on URL or OS-level failures; returns a (status, body) tuple.

symbols.does: Checks whether a supervisor session is still live.
symbols.description: Queries /sessions and returns True only if the named session exists and its state is not 'closed'; returns False if the supervisor is unreachable.

symbols.does: Polls until a supervisor session reaches idle state.
symbols.description: Loops over /sessions waiting for the clone session state to become 'idle', raising CloneError on timeout or if the session closes.

symbols.does: Returns the shell command for an operator to launch an interactive channel-member clone.
symbols.description: Builds a cd + env + claude --resume command with the channel MCP config and development channels; intended for human execution, not agent auto-launch.

symbols.does: Constructs the orient+reflect message sent to a newly woken clone.
symbols.description: Tells the clone it is a point-in-time materialization, explains the value of its era, and asks it to introduce itself from its cut before receiving a current briefing.

symbols.does: Roughly estimates token count of a materialized transcript.
symbols.description: Sums text lengths of message and tool content and divides by four; returns a safe overestimate, defaulting to above the kimi window on read errors.

symbols.does: Selects an ollama model based on estimated context size and explicit request.
symbols.description: Honors explicit non-kimi models; otherwise keeps kimi-k2.7-code:cloud for contexts at or below 256K tokens and falls back to deepseek-v4-flash:cloud for larger contexts.

symbols.does: Applies the TIM-RULE ollama model pick to a materialized transcript.
symbols.description: Short-circuits on explicit non-kimi requests, otherwise estimates context tokens and delegates to pick_ollama_model_for_context.

symbols.does: Materializes and launches a supervised clone from a source transcript at a cut.
symbols.description: Validates the source, materializes it at the requested point, optionally selects an ollama model, spawns a supervised resume session, waits for idle, registers the clone and channel member, persists a clone record, and returns launch metadata plus the operator command.

symbols.does: Looks up a clone record by handle, supervisor session, or session id.
symbols.description: Scans .data/clones/*.json and returns the first record matching any of the clone identifiers; raises CloneError if none match.

symbols.does: Sends a DM to a supervised clone and returns its reply.
symbols.description: Opens the supervisor /watch stream, posts the message via /inject, folds watch events until 'done', and returns the captured reply or raises CloneError on failure or timeout.

symbols.does: Computes a provenance-stable clone:// address.
symbols.description: Returns 'clone://<source_sid>/<at>' from a clone record; the address is stable across re-spawns of the same era.

symbols.does: Patches a persisted clone record.
symbols.description: Reads .data/clones/<handle>.json, applies the supplied dictionary update, writes it back, and returns the updated record.

symbols.does: Resolves a clone:// address to a clone record.
symbols.description: Iterates the clone registry, computes clone_address for each record, and returns the matching record; raises CloneError if no match.

symbols.does: Runs the reflect-before-brief onboarding protocol on one clone.
symbols.description: Injects the onboarding message to capture a reflection, persists it with the clone address, then optionally injects the bring-current briefing; supports partial phases.

symbols.does: Onboards multiple clones in parallel.
symbols.description: Uses a ThreadPoolExecutor to run onboard_clone concurrently over a list of handles or all live clones, collecting results and errors.

symbols.does: Lists registered supervised clones, optionally pruning dead ones.
symbols.description: Reads .data/clones/*.json and, when prune=True, removes records whose supervisor session is no longer alive.

symbols.does: Tears down a supervised clone and cleans up its artifacts.
symbols.description: Calls supervisor /teardown, optionally deletes the materialized prefix, removes the clone record, and deregisters the channel member.

symbols.does: Materializes a clone prefix and emits the operator launch command without spawning.
symbols.description: Creates a fresh resume prefix for a point-in-time cut and returns the exact interactive launch command for a human operator; does not auto-launch.
